purpose: Canonical machine-readable roadmap for Beta Release planning, phase sequencing, governance questions, tranche sizing, and Admin Dashboard visualization.

current_repo_state.current_navigation_summary: Current position: PrayerAppNew records LOTH through b835653. OOR-C03 is complete with 28 ordinary psalmody appointments entered and mapped. OOR-C04 is complete with 238 ordinary-time contexts bound to those appointment keys. OOR-C05 is partially advanced: 25 temporale psalmody contexts are source-backed and mapped, Easter Sunday is recorded as a rubric-governed exception, 8 Advent appointments remain source-blocked, and 112 temporale psalmody contexts remain. LOTH cadence fields are unchanged because this was public app hardening, not corpus-producing LOTH work.

release_identity.release_model: First release represents each major apostolic tradition family through one governed initial recension or tradition lane, then expands by tradition, jurisdiction, calendar, and depth over time.
non_negotiables: Beta Release is not Byzantine-centered in public presentation.
BCP / Anglican Daily Office is the public-facing focus.
Roman Catholic Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours representation is a Beta Release blocker.
Russian / Slavic Byzantine Horologion is the first Eastern Orthodox recension.
Ethiopian / Tewahedo Sa'atat is the first Oriental Orthodox lane.
East Syriac Hudra is the first Church of the East lane.
Other traditions, jurisdictions, and profiles are later expansions, not first-release requirements.
Mobile onboarding must not present the traditions as a smorgasbord of liturgical options.
Anglicanism is the default grammar and fallback, but the first-use experience must not be Anglican-anxious or denominationally defensive.
Eastern Christian routing must not make Byzantine Christianity normative; Church of the East, Oriental Orthodox, and Eastern Orthodox should be listed in chronological order of differentiation.
Tradition-expansion notification is pastoral service, not marketing.
release_identity.mobile_product_thesis: A design-forward digital breviary that opens like a chapel: Anglican in grammar, catholic and orthodox in inheritance, beautiful because the prayer itself is beautiful.
release_identity.accepted_welcome_copy: The Church's daily prayer, appointed for the hour. Begin where you pray, and let the office lead you.
release_identity.anglican_default_posture: Anglican-first by default grammar and fallback, not Anglican-forward as a self-conscious public posture. The app receives the user, routes them to their tradition, and explains the Anglican center only when needed.

release_doctrine.beta_blocker_definition: A defect, absence, or governance gap that prevents honest first public release under the required major-family representation model.
beta_blockers_include: A required major apostolic family has no honest first-release representation.
A public-facing beta path crashes or fails to render.
A baseline office is advertised as present but lacks enough structure or text to be liturgically honest.
The Admin Dashboard misrepresents project state or release blockers.
Ordinary user flow exposes development placeholders, undefined values, stale implementation language, or hidden silent fallbacks.
Roman Catholic Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours baseline is absent.
not_beta_blockers_if_honestly_deferred: Greek, Antiochian, or other non-Slavic Eastern Orthodox profiles.
Coptic Agpeya as a separate Oriental Orthodox lane, provided Ethiopian is stable as first OO representation.
Armenian, Syriac Orthodox, and other Oriental Orthodox additions beyond the Ethiopian first lane.
1960 Roman Breviary, Benedictine, monastic, or other Latin variants if the first Roman Catholic Divine Office baseline is present.
Full database/typed-element Phase 5 migration, provided release data remains honest and stable.
honesty_rules: Do not hide true corpus gaps as UX polish.
Do not present one jurisdictional recension as the entire tradition family.
Do not conflate Ethiopian Sa'atat with Coptic Agpeya.
Do not treat East Syriac Hudra as missing.
Do not call the Universal Office release-ready while Roman Catholic Divine Office representation is absent.

Do not claim Phase 5 typed architecture is complete until it is pressure-tested against Anglican, Roman Catholic, Byzantine, East Syriac, Ethiopian, and any distinct Coptic Agpeya lane pursued before that milestone.

mobile_onboarding_doctrine.welcome_copy: The Church's daily prayer, appointed for the hour. Begin where you pray, and let the office lead you.

current_routes.Latin Catholic: Liturgy of the Hours / Roman office baseline, subject to source-governance and runtime-delivery enablement

eastern_branch.order_note: Listed in chronological order of differentiation so Byzantine/Eastern Orthodox does not become the implicit norm for all Eastern Christianity.

unsure_branch.copy_rule: Explain the Anglican default only after the user selects I'm not sure; do not surface this as a standing note on the main routing screen.

mobile_onboarding_doctrine.branching_rule: Once the user is in a primary tradition, offer further branching from there only when multiple governed forms exist.

mobile_visual_design_doctrine.core_metaphor: A digital breviary that opens like a chapel.
mobile_visual_design_doctrine.anti_slop_rule: Every visible design decision should arise from liturgical function, theological meaning, ecclesial beauty, or the office's own order, not from generic sacred vibes.
mobile_visual_design_doctrine.beauty_rule: Beauty is not ornament; it is prayer made visible. The app should evoke incense, monastery, cathedral, and Easter Vigil beauty while avoiding cartoon high-church overload and AI sacred luxury.
mobile_visual_design_doctrine.tradition_register_rule: Tradition-specific aesthetics may subtly modify atmosphere, accent, typography, ornamental grammar, and terminology when rooted in that tradition's own prayer, book culture, calendar, or ecclesial memory. They must not become costume skins.

coverage_notice_doctrine.rule: When a selected tradition-family has only partial current coverage, show a calm threshold information screen before routing to the available governed form.
coverage_notice_doctrine.oriental_orthodox_notice: This release currently begins with the Ethiopian tradition. The liturgies of the other Oriental Orthodox churches, including Coptic, Armenian, West Syriac / Malankara, and Eritrean traditions, will be included in future updates of The Universal Office.
coverage_notice_doctrine.eastern_orthodox_notice: This release currently begins with the Byzantine Horologion, with English-language coverage slanted toward Slavic usage. Future updates will broaden jurisdictional and local-use support, including Greek, Antiochian, OCA, ROCOR, and other received Orthodox usages where source-governed material is available.

notification_signup.primary_label: Notify me when this tradition expands
notification_signup.rule: The signup must be opt-in, visually secondary, scoped to the selected tradition-family, and framed as service rather than marketing.

browser_qc_doctrine.release_allowlist_rule: scripts/ remains excluded from release packaging by default; browser-runtime QC scripts may be explicitly allowlisted when they must run from deployed pages.
browser_qc_doctrine.codespaces_auth_rule: Codespaces tunnel/auth/CORS failures are deployment/test-target failures, not app runtime failures, unless the requested QC JavaScript is actually returned and executed.

major_tradition_families.blocker_reason: The BCP path is the public-facing beta center and must pass public-flow hardening.

major_tradition_families.first_release_lane: Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours
major_tradition_families.blocker_reason: Roman Catholic Divine Office / Liturgy of the Hours baseline remains a Beta Release blocker. The external Roman LOTH control corpus is being built first in JWJeffery/LOTH; a public Roman-family derivative lane will be built afterward using the source-governed corpus as structural and liturgical guide, subject to source-status/source-clearing.

The current working strategy is external Roman LOTH first, public Roman-family derivative second. JWJeffery/LOTH now contains source governance, source-status registry, office structure, resolver maps, Night Prayer weekly-cycle population, Grail 1963 psalter corpus resource, psalm-numbering registry, source supplement policy, and Universalis comparison tooling. external Roman LOTH corpus work may proceed while deployable-source selection remains unresolved. Official/current LOTH text must not be exposed publicly, fetched by PrayerAppNew, or rendered externally until a deployable corpus lane enables it. Broad resolved Office of Readings date-block snapshots are no longer the forward architecture. The bundle remains non-materializing: refs/citationRefs only, no text-unit writes, and no broad daily date-block snapshots. The resolver emits complete ordered 11-slot composition maps for three pilot contexts while remaining non-materializing: unitRefs/citationRefs only, no text-unit writes, and no broad daily date-block snapshots. Office of Readings full metadata slot-binding bundle closed in LOTH commits e0bab4a, 7c8bbd8, f237a42, and 2ba1bb2: second responsory, Te Deum rule, collect rule, hymn, and verse are now bound into the metadata composition pilot. The metadata resolver now has all 11 Office of Readings slots resolved by refs/rules or rule-not-applicable for the pilot contexts, while remaining non-materializing: no liturgical text, no Office text-unit writes, and no broad daily date-block snapshots. Office of Readings governed text-unit hydration bundle closed in LOTH commits e2b51ca, 293f33a, 91c3039, and d46268b: complete metadata pilot audit, stable ordinary text units, populated text-unit hydration, and Te Deum reusable unit population are now recorded. OOR has pivoted from metadata-only assembly into governed reusable text-unit population. Opening versicle, conclusion, and Te Deum are populated as reusable root units and hydrated where applicable; no daily date-block snapshots or broad OOR materializations are authorized. Office of Readings hymn/verse candidate text-unit bundle closed in LOTH commits 003478d, b448eb3, and 066d49f: text-population eligibility gate added, candidate hymn units populated, and candidate verse units populated. OOR now hydrates opening versicle, hymn, verse, and conclusion across pilot contexts, plus Te Deum where applicable. Hymn and verse texts are explicitly candidate public-domain/traditional-English lanes pending final LOTH alignment and are not asserted as current official LOTH appointments. Office of Readings collect/responsory candidate text-unit bundle closed in LOTH commits 7b96f24, 0a98778, 9b54a97, and 3ca5dbd: candidate collect units populated; remaining source-gated refs report added/refreshed; candidate first responsory units populated; candidate second responsory units populated. OOR now hydrates opening versicle, hymn, verse, first responsory, second responsory, collect, and conclusion across ordinary/Lenten pilot contexts, plus Te Deum in the solemnity context. Eligibility now records 8 populated slots and 3 source-gated slots: psalmody, first reading, and second reading remain gated. Office of Readings metadata pilot population completed in LOTH commits 96253d7, 794f624, and a4849c8: candidate first-reading units populated, candidate second-reading units populated, and candidate psalmody units populated. The OOR metadata pilot now has 11 populated slots and 0 source-gated slots. Hydration now reports 10 hydrated slots for ordinary/Lenten pilot contexts, with Te Deum rule-not-applicable, and 11 hydrated slots for the solemnity context. This completes the metadata pilot population only; it does not authorize broad daily date-block snapshots, runtime delivery, or full OOR cycle completion.
major_tradition_families.runtime_disabled_note: Roman LOTH runtime delivery in PrayerAppNew remains disabled until a source-status/source-clearing record authorizes external display. PrayerAppNew must not fetch from the private repo or expose official/current LOTH text publicly before authorization. A later public Roman-family derivative lane may be built from authorized or public-domain materials after the external Roman LOTH control corpus establishes the structural guide.

phase_plan.title: Roadmap extraction and mission reset
phase_plan.purpose: Create the roadmap source of truth and stop coding against stale Byzantine-centered release assumptions.
phase_plan.exit_gate: project_roadmap.json exists, validates, and accurately encodes BCP-facing beta identity, major-family representation requirements, Roman Catholic release blocker status, and phase/tranche doctrine.

phase_plan.purpose: Make the Admin Dashboard visualize the whole Universal Office beta roadmap instead of only the Byzantine release lane.

phase_plan.title: BCP public-facing hardening
phase_plan.purpose: Harden the Anglican / BCP Daily Office path because it is the public-facing center of Beta Release.

tranche_plan.notes: Office of Readings source-model failed-course correction closed in LOTH commits 9409041, be68b90, and 10d93fa. The previous broad DivineOffice-derived daily date-block population model was retired from active corpus. The active forward path is controlled Universalis appointment/structure discovery plus local Grail psalm-body materialization, followed by cycle/proper/resolver architecture for reusable units. Broad resolved daily Office of Readings snapshots must not resume. LOTH commit d9fdea1 closed the Office of Readings cycle/proper/resolver architecture plan. This is a non-materializing architecture contract: 11 slots, 11 resolver layers, composition-map output, unitRefs-only resolution, no official text emission, no Office text-unit writes, and no broad daily date-block snapshots. Office of Readings post-architecture metadata/cycle-map bundle closed in LOTH commits fa10584, f530cd5, 8ae8a49, and e7171d3. This bundle added a metadata composition pilot, a refs-only psalmody cycle-map skeleton, binding from psalmody appointment-map refs into the composition pilot, and a citation/ref-only biblical first-reading cycle-map skeleton. The bundle writes no liturgical text, no Office text units, and no broad daily date-block snapshots. Office of Readings composition-resolver bundle closed in LOTH commits a4a9761, 503ebdc, dff67ea, and 0367a90. This bundle bound first_reading slots to biblical readingKey/citationRefs, added the metadata composition resolver, bound first_responsory slots to paired responsory refs, and bound second_reading slots to secondReadingKey/citation/source refs. The resolver now emits complete ordered 11-slot Office of Readings composition maps for the pilot contexts while preserving unresolved slots explicitly. No liturgical text, Office text units, or broad daily date-block snapshots are written. Office of Readings full metadata slot-binding bundle closed in LOTH commits e0bab4a, 7c8bbd8, f237a42, and 2ba1bb2. This bundle bound second_responsory slots to paired second-reading refs, bound Te Deum by rule, bound collect by rule, and bound hymn/verse by metadata refs. The resolver now has all 11 pilot slots resolved or rule-not-applicable without emitting liturgical text or writing Office text units/date blocks. Office of Readings governed text-unit hydration bundle closed in LOTH commits e2b51ca, 293f33a, 91c3039, and d46268b. The complete metadata pilot audit passes with 3 resolutions, 11 slots each, 0 unresolved; opening versicle and conclusion are populated as reusable stable ordinary units; the populated text-unit hydrator now hydrates reusable units into OOR pilot output; and the Te Deum reusable unit is populated and hydrated where applicable. This does not authorize runtime delivery or daily date-block materialization. Office of Readings candidate hymn/verse population bundle closed in LOTH commits 003478d, b448eb3, and 066d49f. Eligibility now records 5 populated slots and 6 source-gated slots. Hydration now includes hymn and verse in all pilot contexts, with Te Deum additionally hydrated where applicable. Hymn and verse lanes are candidate public-domain/traditional-English/source-safe lanes pending final LOTH alignment, not official current appointment assertions. Office of Readings collect/responsory population bundle closed in LOTH commits 7b96f24, 0a98778, 9b54a97, and 3ca5dbd. Eligibility now records 8 populated slots and 3 source-gated slots. Hydration now includes collect, first responsory, and second responsory in all pilot contexts. The remaining source-gated slots are psalmody, first reading, and second reading. Office of Readings final metadata-pilot population bundle closed in LOTH commits 96253d7, 794f624, and a4849c8. Eligibility now records 11 populated slots and 0 source-gated slots. Hydration now includes first reading, second reading, and psalmody in all pilot contexts. Ordinary and Lenten pilot contexts hydrate 10 slots because Te Deum is rule-not-applicable; solemnity hydrates all 11 slots. This completes OOR metadata pilot population only.

governance_questions.question: What source/copyright strategy governs the Roman Catholic Divine Office baseline?
governance_questions.resolution: Private JWJeffery/LOTH corpus/governance repo holds source identification, source-status records, architecture maps, and synthetic resolver. external Roman LOTH corpus work may continue while deployable-source selection remains unresolved. PrayerAppNew runtime delivery remains disabled until a deployable Roman corpus lane is selected.

governance_questions.question: What is the minimum Roman Catholic Divine Office baseline that counts as honest Beta Release representation?
governance_questions.notes: Synthetic resolver proves office architecture, not a public baseline. Minimum baseline still requires source-status-authorized text/rendering decision.

governance_questions.question: What defects, if any, remain in the BCP-facing public beta path?
What exact Ethiopian Sa'atat depth is required to represent Oriental Orthodoxy honestly in Beta Release?
What exact East Syriac Hudra depth is required to represent Church of the East honestly in Beta Release?
What source-governed policy should control Polyeleos / rank selection and Psalms 134-135 corpus?
How should annual fixed/movable Typikon collisions be handled across EO old/new calendar modes?
When are the major office families sufficiently represented to begin typed-element/database migration pressure-testing?

How should the Roman office engine disclose liturgical composition, handle printed-source navigation, and remain source-edition-agnostic across current restricted and future public-domain corpus tracks?
governance_questions.resolution: Composition map accepted as a core liturgical-assembly disclosure model. Ribbon placement accepted as source-navigation metadata and physical-book expression of liturgical composition. Current LOTH implementation remains an external architecture-prototype track. After internal LOTH stabilization, a future public-domain Roman-family corpus track will reuse the Roman office engine and composition-map architecture. Roman office engine must remain source-edition-aware — distinguishing office-family architecture, source edition, calendar/rubrical regime, textual corpus, navigation affordances, source status, and public/runtime delivery status — and must not be hardwired to current current LOTH text.

governance_questions.question: After the Roman Office build lane stabilizes, should Universal Office add a cross-corpus source-posture ledger covering all major office families?
governance_questions.defer_until: After active Roman LOTH baseline build work is no longer the immediate Phase 3 bottleneck.
governance_questions.notes: Roman LOTH requires unusually strict source-status gating because it is a modern external source-governed corpus. That standard should eventually inform a wider source-posture ledger, but app-wide source-posture management must not interrupt the current Roman Office build lane. Future work should classify source posture across corpus families without treating every tradition as if it has the same legal/source-status problem.
